name: Success check confirmation (browser-native)
algorithm: createSuccessCheck builds a small role=status element. show(message) sets the text, unhides, and unless reduced motion is set replays the draw by toggling is-animating (a forced reflow re-triggers the keyframes). CSS draws the ring then the tick via stroke-dashoffset and fades the message up, then it auto-hides after lingerMs. The default resting CSS state shows the check fully drawn so it is never left half-drawn.
reduced_motion: No stroke draw, fade, or movement under prefers-reduced-motion; the check shows already complete with static message.
responsive_behaviour: inline-flex; fixed 40px SVG mark, message reflows; no layout-affecting motion
browser_support: All modern browsers; SVG stroke-dash animation and matchMedia universally supported; SSR-guarded.
accessibility: role=status / aria-live polite announces the message once without stealing focus; SVG mark aria-hidden; success conveyed by tick shape + text not colour; deliberately short and quiet (no confetti).
